title: Governance Event Bus Foundation
purpose: Create a unified governance event bus that captures runtime actions as an append-only event stream. This provides the observability substrate, debugging substrate, telemetry substrate, and future orchestration substrate for the governance runtime.
architectural_constraints: Governance events are separate from application domain events
Event bus uses append-only JSONL logs (not a queue system)
Events must validate against governance-event.schema.json before emit
Retention policy: 90 days info, 1 year warn/error, indefinite critical
Event emitter must resolve correlation_id from active execution context

deliverables.description: JSON Schema for governance events (event_id, timestamp, type, severity, category, payload)
Registry of all governance event types with categories, severities, descriptions
Retention, sampling, and severity rules for event streams
CLI emitter: validates event, appends to daily JSONL, updates index
CLI query tool: filter by time, category, severity, ticket, milestone
Event validation module used by emitter
Daily append-only JSONL logs with filtered streams subdirectory

acceptance_criteria: governance-event.schema.json validates all critical event types
event-catalog.json defines 11+ event types across 8 categories
emit-governance-event.ts appends valid events to daily JSONL
query-governance-events.ts filters by category, severity, ticket
Event stream policy defines retention and sampling rules
Existing JSON protocols (lifecycle, checkpoint, state-mutation) declare event emissions
No duplicate event types between governance and application event buses

failure_modes.scenario: Event bus becomes write bottleneck during high-frequency operations
failure_modes.mitigation: Batch emits, async append, throttle non-critical events

failure_modes.scenario: Event log grows unbounded without retention
failure_modes.mitigation: Enforce retention policy with automated cleanup
Payload schema restricts fields; no aggregate IDs in governance events

metadata.operational_rationale: Runtime state, audits, and checkpoints exist but there is no unified event stream. This explains silent failures, invisible runtime faults, poor observability, weak debugging ergonomics, and difficult recovery analysis.
